key_insights.insight: Pelvic floor dysfunction can manifest in various ways, including pain during sex, urinary issues, and even erectile dysfunction, highlighting its interconnectedness with overall health.
key_insights.quote: So there's a whole host of issues that can come from having pelvic floor abnormalities. And even if you've had, say, lower back injuries or hip injuries, they can affect the coordination of the pelvic floor because it's all interconnected.

key_insights.insight: The average time for intercourse (penetration to male climax) is around 5.1 to 5.7 minutes, a duration often shorter than what people perceive or desire.
key_insights.quote: 5.1 to 5.7 minutes depending on the study you look at and they they looked at all commerce so it's actually different in different countries.

key_insights.insight: Testosterone is crucial for desire in both men and women, and its production is heavily impacted by factors like sleep, stress, and diet.
key_insights.quote: Testosterone in women is more predominant than actually estrogen. We have more testosterone in our bodies than we do estrogen. And testosterone is very important for desire in both men and women.

key_insights.insight: Ejaculating 21 times or more a month may lower the risk of developing prostate cancer.
key_insights.quote: men who ejaculated 21 times or more a month were less likely to develop prostate cancer.

key_insights.insight: Much of the understanding about sex comes from unreliable sources like porn, setting unrealistic expectations and potentially causing dissatisfaction in real-life encounters.
key_insights.quote: if people are learning about sex through porn,porn is entertainment. It is doctored, it is not real. And I think people then take that and they say, why did not my sexual encounter not look like that?

key_insights.insight: While many people think of sex as a way to reach a certain quota, the quality of sex trumps the quantity.
key_insights.quote: It's not the quantity of sex that matters. It's the quality of sex, right?

key_insights.insight: Doctors should be asking patients about their sexual health, because issues can lead to pervasive shame, relationship problems, and potentially signal underlying health conditions like heart disease.
key_insights.quote: Even when you go to see your primary doctor, when was the last time they asked you about anything? They might ask you about erections, but that's probably where it ends, right? Even if they ask about that, they're definitely not asking women, are you having orgasms?

actionable_advice.advice: Practice pelvic floor exercises (Kegels) to strengthen pelvic floor muscles.
actionable_advice.how_to: Squeeze, contract and relax the muscles you use to stop your urine stream for 5 seconds, relax for 5 seconds. Start lying down, then progress to sitting and standing.

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule 'intimacy time' with your partner to prioritize physical connection without the pressure of having sex.
actionable_advice.how_to: Put time on the calendar to be together, physically present (no phones), touching each other, maybe getting undressed, without the expectation of sex.

actionable_advice.advice: If you have low testosterone, prioritize sleep, engage in resistance exercise, and follow a Mediterranean diet.
actionable_advice.how_to: Aim for 7-9 hours of quality sleep, do resistance exercises targeting large muscle groups, and eat a diet rich in vegetables, fruits, healthy fats, and low in processed foods.
Avoid drinking from plastic water bottles and warming food in plastic, opting for glass containers instead.

actionable_advice.advice: Communicate openly with your partner about sex and sexual health, even if the conversations are uncomfortable.
actionable_advice.how_to: Have conversations outside the bedroom. Focus on 'I' statements, discuss feelings and desires, and be patient with the process.

memorable_stories: An anecdote about a 70-year-old woman with long-term pelvic floor dysfunction causing bladder issues highlights the lasting impact of unresolved trauma.
Personal story of acting strange in a relationship due to insecurities and lack of communication, leading to misunderstandings and sexlessness.
A story of women having 95% orgasm rate with other women compared to 45% with men during initial sexual encounters, which highlights the educational disconnection
contrarian_claims: Lubricant is for everybody. It can make things more fun and slippery. It makes things more enjoyable. It certainly makes pain less of an issue for a lot of people.
There's no need for men to abstain from masturbation prior to athletic events. Data has not been found to improve performance when abstaining from ejaculation
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Better-Sex Doctor: The Link Between Masturbating & Prostate Cancer! This Is The Perfect Amount Of Times To Have Sex! Strong Pelvic Floor = Better Sex! Dr Rena Malik
